top_signals.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
top_signals.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India Ramps Up Share Sale Plan to Cushion Budget Hit by Oil
top_signals.summary: India is fast-tracking efforts to sell stakes in some of its largest state-owned companies, including the nation’s biggest life insurer, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s administration looks to bolster public finances strained by high oil prices.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Trump-backed American Bitcoin sets 1-for-15 reverse stock split in bid to maintain Nasdaq listing
top_signals.summary: American Bitcoin's reverse split will reduce outstanding shares from roughly 1.09 billion to about 73 million.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Piero Cipollone: Central bank money for the digital era
Policy paper: Green Book Review 2025: One year on
top_signals.summary: Report outlining progress in implementing the actions arising from the Green Book Review 2025.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, January 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income increased $113.8 billion (0.4 percent at a monthly rate) in January, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis. Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-increased $219.9 billion (0.9 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $81.1 billion (0.4 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $85.8 billion in January. Personal saving was $1.05 trillion in January, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of disposable personal income-was 4.5 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Exclusive | JPMorgan, Citi and Big Banks Plan New Tokenized Deposit System to Answer Crypto - WSJ
top_signals.summary: Exclusive | JPMorgan, Citi and Big Banks Plan New Tokenized Deposit System to Answer Crypto  WSJ
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Outdoor Recreation Economic Statistics, U.S. and States, 2024
top_signals.summary: The value added of the outdoor recreation economy accounted for 2.4 percent ($696.7 billion) of current-dollar gross domestic product (GDP) for the nation in 2024. Across states and the District of Columbia, value added for outdoor recreation as a share of state GDP ranged from 6.1 percent in Hawaii to 1.0 percent in the District of Columbia. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: France’s far right pledges 40 million air conditioners as heat turns political
top_signals.summary: Across Europe air conditioning is a new battleground as officials grapple with how to adapt aging infrastructure to debilitating heat as the climate changes.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
